label: law-sharp
headline_statement: Confirmed after object correction: write \(\mu_{1h}(P)=\E_P[Y_1\mid D=d_h]\) and \(\beta_h(P)=\mu_{1h}(P)-\tau_h(P)\), define \(\mathcal A_L^{\mathrm{mc}}(P)=\{\alpha\in\mathcal A_L(P):\underline y\le \beta_h(P)+\alpha_{jh}\le\overline y\ \forall h,\ \forall j\ne h\}\), and set \(L_h^{\mathrm{mc}}(L;P)=\min_{\alpha\in\mathcal A_L^{\mathrm{mc}}(P)}\theta_h(\alpha)\), \(U_h^{\mathrm{mc}}(L;P)=\max_{\alpha\in\mathcal A_L^{\mathrm{mc}}(P)}\theta_h(\alpha)\). Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:grid-bounds}--\ref{ass:completion}, with the algebraic object replaced by this mean-completion polytope, \([L_h^{\mathrm{mc}}(L;P),U_h^{\mathrm{mc}}(L;P)]\) is law-sharp for the finite-grid ACRT over latent completions preserving \(P\) whose off-arm potential outcomes are assigned by finite \([\underline y,\overline y]\)-supported product kernels conditional on the observed data: every such completion maps into \(\mathcal A_L^{\mathrm{mc}}(P)\), and every \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_L^{\mathrm{mc}}(P)\) is realized by such a completion.
lineage_note: Original law-sharp algebraic envelope refuted by a three-dose binary boundary witness with alpha_21=1 implying E[Y1(d2)|D=d1]=2; mended to law-sharpness of the mean-completion polytope using mu_1h(P)-tau_h(P), with finite support required only for off-arm kernels conditional on the observed data. Completion convention justified by Molinari2020, arXiv:2004.11751 / DOI 10.48550/arXiv.2004.11751.
